title: Quiz: QFT Applications
description: Test your understanding of QFT applications in quantum algorithms

questions.question: In Quantum Phase Estimation (QPE), which QFT operation is used?
questions.explanation: QPE uses the inverse QFT at the end to convert the phase-encoded state into a measurement-ready computational basis state.

questions.question: How does QFT help in period finding?

options.text: It converts periodic states into peaked probability distributions

questions.explanation: QFT transforms a periodic superposition into peaks at positions corresponding to N/period, revealing the period.

questions.question: In Shor's algorithm, QFT is used to:

options.text: Find the period of modular exponentiation

questions.explanation: Shor's algorithm reduces factoring to period finding. QFT reveals the period of the function aˣ mod N.

questions.question: In QFT-based quantum addition, what operation performs the 'add b' step?

options.text: Applying phase rotations in Fourier basis

questions.explanation: In Fourier basis, addition is performed by applying phase rotations proportional to b.

questions.question: The QFT provides exponential speedup in Shor's algorithm for factoring.
questions.explanation: Classical factoring takes exponential time; Shor's with QFT takes polynomial time—an exponential speedup.
